title: Snap Q4 2022 Earnings Results
text:
Snap Q4 2022 Earnings Results

Financial Performance

Snap reported Q4 2022 revenue of $1.3 billion, representing essentially flat year-over-year growth of just 0.1%, marking the company's lowest quarterly growth as a public entity. The company posted a net loss of $288.5 million for the quarter, including $34 million in restructuring charges, compared to net income of $23 million in the prior year. Adjusted earnings per share came in at 14 cents, beating analyst expectations of 11 cents.

User Growth

Despite revenue struggles, Snapchat's daily active users reached 375 million in Q4, up from 363 million in Q3 and representing 17% year-over-year growth.

Forward Outlook

The company declined to provide specific Q1 guidance, citing uncertainties related to the operating environment. However, management indicated internal forecasts assume Q1 revenue will decline 2-10% year-over-year, with actual performance through January 31 showing approximately 7% quarter-to-date decline.

CEO Commentary

CEO Evan Spiegel acknowledged the company faces significant headwinds as it works to improve advertiser returns and deepen user engagement.

Market Reaction

Snap shares fell more than 14% in after-hours trading following the earnings announcement.
